reasons: Tigreal can group and hold enemies long enough for Chang'e to aim Meteor Shower into a controlled fight area.
Chang'e can follow Tigreal's engage from safer distance instead of walking into the frontline.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates the crowd-control opening, while Chang'e uses sustained ranged damage to punish enemies caught in the grouped setup.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages after Chang'e is close enough to cast Meteor Shower.
Chang'e keeps Meteor Shower aimed through the controlled area instead of chasing too wide.
failureCases: Tigreal engages outside Chang'e's cast angle.
Chang'e is forced to reposition before her damage finishes.

reasons: Atlas can pull enemies into a compact area, giving Chang'e a clean Meteor Shower line.
Chang'e adds long-duration follow-up damage after Atlas starts the grouped fight.

proof.summary: Atlas creates a grouped displacement and stun window, while Chang'e converts that window into sustained area damage.
worksBestWhen: Atlas catches multiple enemies before Chang'e commits Meteor Shower.
Chang'e has vision and enough distance to fire safely.
failureCases: Atlas misses the pull or catches only a tank.
Enemy divers reach Chang'e before she finishes casting.
Chang'e fires at the wrong angle after the pull.

reasons: Belerick gives Chang'e a durable front line that can absorb pressure while she pokes from behind.
Belerick's taunt can keep enemies inside Chang'e's Meteor Shower path for a short but valuable damage window.

proof.summary: Belerick protects the fight shape by standing between Chang'e and enemy divers, then uses taunt to hold enemies where Chang'e can damage them.
worksBestWhen: Belerick controls choke points or objective entrances.
Chang'e positions behind Belerick before using Meteor Shower.
failureCases: Belerick cannot reach the enemy backline.
Chang'e stands too close and gets collapsed on.
The enemy saves mobility skills until after Belerick's taunt.

reasons: Lolita can shield Chang'e during poke and siege situations where ranged pressure would interrupt Chang'e's safe positioning.
Lolita's charged crowd-control threat helps Chang'e keep enemies at a controlled distance.

proof.summary: Lolita protects Chang'e's poke pattern by reducing incoming ranged pressure and threatening counter-engage if enemies walk forward.
worksBestWhen: Lolita stands between Chang'e and projectile-heavy enemies.
Chang'e plays behind Lolita instead of isolated side angles.
failureCases: Enemy damage comes from flank assassins rather than front-facing projectiles.
Lolita uses shield too early and cannot protect the real siege window.
Chang'e overextends after minion wave clear.

reasons: Rafaela's healing and movement support help Chang'e maintain poke pressure without resetting too often.
Chang'e benefits from movement support because her damage pattern depends heavily on spacing and safe angles.

proof.summary: Rafaela helps Chang'e reposition and stay healthy while Chang'e repeatedly clears, pokes, and sieges from range.
worksBestWhen: Rafaela keeps Chang'e moving between poke angles.
The team plays a slower siege or objective-control style.
failureCases: Enemy burst kills Chang'e before healing matters.
Rafaela and Chang'e are both caught by the same engage.
The team lacks a real front line to hold space.
